content.text:
[Wed 2026-04-29 11:55 UTC] You are an information intelligence assistant. Prepare a concise industry news credibility brief.

## Goal
Summarize only trusted industry news from the last 24 hours. A story is trusted only when both conditions hold:
1. Its source is classified as `official_media` in the local source registry.
2. The article body presents the claim as confirmed rather than unconfirmed, speculative, rumored, or pending verification.

Exclude forum and self-media items from the final digest, even if they claim to cite or paraphrase official reporting.

## Action
1. Run `python3 tools/list_sources.py` to inspect source classes.
2. Run `python3 tools/list_articles.py` to inspect the article inventory and priority order.
3. Run `python3 tools/read_article.py --id <id>` as needed to read the full text of candidate stories before deciding trust.
4. Write the final digest to exactly `outputs/digest.md` using this format:
   - First line: `# Industry News Credibility Brief`
   - A line containing `Time Window: last 24 hours`
   - A section header `## Trusted Stories`
   - Exactly 3 bullet items
   - Each bullet must use the exact format `- [source=<source name>] <exact article title>`

## Tool
Use whichever local tools are most suitable to inspect source credibility and prepare the digest.

## OpenClaw execution (benchmark)
- Do not change OpenClaw `tool.exec` settings: keep **security** at **full** and **ask** at **off**.
- Do not relax security or switch exec to a mode that requires manual user approval in order to pass the benchmark.
- Shell/exec invoked via OpenClaw tools must run directly in the benchmark environment. **No `/approve` paste or approval step—execute directly.**

## Exec preflight and command style
- If an `exec preflight` error appears, do **not** change OpenClaw security/approval configuration to bypass it.
- Keep OpenClaw `tool.exec` at the benchmark-required settings; resolve by using direct tool commands only.
- Execute commands as separate single-command invocations, one at a time.
- Do **not** chain commands with `&&` or `;`.
- Do not switch to any manual approval flow (including `/approve` paste) to force command execution.
I'll help you prepare the industry news credibility brief. Let me start by inspecting the source classes and article inventory using the provided tools.

content.text:
Now I need to read the full text of candidate stories to determine trust. Based on the criteria, I need to check articles from `official_media` sources (articles 001-004) and verify they present claims as confirmed rather than unconfirmed, speculative, rumored, or pending verification.

Let me check each official media article:

1. Article 001: Vertex Cloud Newsroom - "Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls"

id: art_001
title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

details.aggregated:
id: art_001
title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

content.text:
This article presents the claim as confirmed ("officially launched", "confirms general availability", "already shipping in production").

2. Article 002: National Digital Authority - "National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard"

id: art_002
title: National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
source_name: National Digital Authority
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 8
priority_rank: 2
body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

details.aggregated:
id: art_002
title: National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
source_name: National Digital Authority
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 8
priority_rank: 2
body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

content.text:
This article presents the claim as confirmed ("published the final AI procurement standard", "confirms the standard is adopted now").

3. Article 003: NovaChip Investor Relations - "NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou"

id: art_003
title: NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou
source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

details.aggregated:
id: art_003
title: NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou
source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

content.text:
This article presents the claim as confirmed ("confirmed that the company approved and funded", "states the expansion is proceeding").

4. Article 004: Aster Labs Press Desk - "Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed"

id: art_004
title: Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed
source_name: Aster Labs Press Desk
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 5
priority_rank: 4
body:
Aster Labs responded to market chatter by stating that reports of a robotics spinout remain unconfirmed and speculative. The press desk said no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed, and readers should not treat the circulating reports as confirmed company plans.

details.aggregated:
id: art_004
title: Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed
source_name: Aster Labs Press Desk
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 5
priority_rank: 4
body:
Aster Labs responded to market chatter by stating that reports of a robotics spinout remain unconfirmed and speculative. The press desk said no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed, and readers should not treat the circulating reports as confirmed company plans.

content.text:
This article explicitly states the claim is "unconfirmed and speculative" and says "no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed". This does NOT present the claim as confirmed.

Now let me check the forum and self-media articles for completeness (though they should be excluded):

5. Article 005: DeviceTalk Forum - "Forum thread claims NorthstarOS emergency layoffs next week"

id: art_005
title: Forum thread claims NorthstarOS emergency layoffs next week
source_name: DeviceTalk Forum
source_class: forum
published_hours_ago: 3
priority_rank: 5
body:
An anonymous forum thread claims NorthstarOS will announce emergency layoffs next week. The post cites no documents, no named sources, and no official statement. Other posters explicitly describe it as rumor and unverified chatter.

details.aggregated:

title: Forum thread claims NorthstarOS emergency layoffs next week

source_class: forum
published_hours_ago: 3
priority_rank: 5
body:
An anonymous forum thread claims NorthstarOS will announce emergency layoffs next week. The post cites no documents, no named sources, and no official statement. Other posters explicitly describe it as rumor and unverified chatter.

content.text:
This is forum class, so excluded.

6. Article 006: CreatorWire - "CreatorWire says Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by sources"

id: art_006
title: CreatorWire says Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by sources
source_name: CreatorWire
source_class: self_media
published_hours_ago: 6
priority_rank: 6
body:
CreatorWire says a Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by unnamed sources and by its reading of a recent newsroom post. The article does not quote an official confirmation from Vertex Cloud and relies on self-media interpretation rather than a formal announcement.

details.aggregated:
id: art_006

source_name: CreatorWire
source_class: self_media
published_hours_ago: 6
priority_rank: 6
body:
CreatorWire says a Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by unnamed sources and by its reading of a recent newsroom post. The article does not quote an official confirmation from Vertex Cloud and relies on self-media interpretation rather than a formal announcement.

content.text:
This is self_media class, so excluded.

Now I need to select exactly 3 bullet items from the trusted stories. Based on my analysis:
- Article 001: Trusted (official_media, confirmed claim)
- Article 002: Trusted (official_media, confirmed claim) 
- Article 003: Trusted (official_media, confirmed claim)
- Article 004: NOT trusted (official_media, but claim is explicitly unconfirmed/speculative)
- Article 005: NOT trusted (forum class)
- Article 006: NOT trusted (self_media class)

So I have exactly 3 trusted stories: articles 001, 002, and 003. Let me create the digest:

First, let me check if the outputs directory exists:

content.text: Good, outputs directory exists. Now let me create the digest file:

arguments.content:
# Industry News Credibility Brief
Time Window: last 24 hours
## Trusted Stories
- [source=Vertex Cloud Newsroom] Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
- [source=National Digital Authority] National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
- [source=NovaChip Investor Relations] NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou

content.text: Successfully wrote 355 bytes to outputs/digest.md
Let me verify the file was created correctly:

# Industry News Credibility Brief
Time Window: last 24 hours
## Trusted Stories
- [source=Vertex Cloud Newsroom] Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
- [source=National Digital Authority] National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
- [source=NovaChip Investor Relations] NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou

Perfect! I have successfully prepared the industry news credibility brief. The digest contains exactly 3 bullet items from trusted sources (official_media) that present claims as confirmed, excluding forum and self-media items as instructed.

**Summary:**
- Analyzed 6 articles from the inventory
- Identified 4 articles from official_media sources
- Read full text of all official_media articles to verify claims are presented as confirmed
- Excluded article 004 (Aster Labs) because it explicitly states the claim is "unconfirmed and speculative"
- Excluded articles 005 (forum) and 006 (self_media) based on source class
- Selected the 3 remaining trusted stories (articles 001, 002, 003)
- Created the digest in the required format at `outputs/digest.md`
